[2:10:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Weekly Syncs Structure"   NÚMEROS:  3 syncs por semana, treinamento diário   VISUAL:  Estrutura de reuniões semanais com a equipe   [2:10:48]  "All right. All right. So, I think the last thing here was uh yeah, last last uh I'm going to say this and we'll just skip the rest because there's like two more slides anyways. Uh people just usually want to know how we run our secretly weekly syncs."   [2:11:08]  "We have training every single day on a company level and then uh we have three syncs a week like actual syncs like they do glove touches and things like that but actual syncs with every individual team three times a week."   [2:11:28]  "The first one of the week is focused on pipeline numbers and goals for the week. So they'll they'll do everything I just said. They'll show them the priority buckets. Accountabil uh visibility is accountability's best friend. So we'll actually share our screen in front of all the closers. We'll show a dashboard. We'll show the leaderboards."   [2:11:52]  "We'll tell them where they need to be. We'll kind of, you know, game tape. The competition is awesome. Um the second call is usually call review and game tape. So it's just specialized training to that offer."   [2:12:10]  "And then the third sync of the week is usually product knowledge or what we call brand dynamics, which is sales training specific to the product knowledge, right? It's like it's brand specific objection handling and things like that. So, that's how we run the three syncs."   [2:12:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "What Syncs Are NOT"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Lista do que evitar em reuniões de equipe   [2:12:38]  "Um, I'll just tell you what syncs aren't. A lot of sales managers make this mistake. They treat it as like a glove touch. They fuck for 30 minutes. You know, how are things going? They ask about their weekend."   [2:12:55]  "Like, I'm not saying don't build culture and don't have that type of camaraderie, but like you're there to make money and the conversation should be focused around money-making type of things, right?"   [2:13:10]  "Um, it's not like an admin time. It's not a long motivational dialogue or monologue. What they are is the most important culture touch point, right? I don't know about you guys, my whole operation is virtual. We have reps in like every single state in a couple different countries, right?"   [2:13:32]  "So, literally the only place we build culture is Slack and Zoom. And by the way, you better believe I literally have an SOP that tells our sales managers they have to use emojis in Slack."   [2:13:48]  "Like I've learned these things, guys, because if you're in Slack and somebody's like a really short written text communicator, I can't tell you how many times we've had a rep set on set up a one-on-one and they're like, 'Hey dude, I think my manager is pissed at me.'"   [2:14:08]  "And we'll ask the evidence to support it and it will be like a text that didn't have an emoji. So, it's like it's stupid and it seems petty, but if Slack is one of the main places we build culture, we have to be intentional about how we communicate there, right?"   [2:14:28]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "What Syncs ARE"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Propósito real das reuniões de equipe   [2:14:32]  "So, Slack and Zoom calls, we create healthy competition, focus on revenue impacting items. Uh, we go over opportunities for the week to strengthen the team. We go over the validation goals of the hyperspecific things that they should be focused on, things like that."   [2:14:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Levels of Leverage"   NÚMEROS:  4 níveis de alavancagem   VISUAL:  Hierarquia de criação de leverage na organização   [2:15:00]  "And then the last thing I was going to say is leverage. You have to teach your team uh where does it say right here. You have to teach your team. I actually have one more slide that you guys will want to know, which is how you know if your sales manager is doing a good job."   [2:15:20]  "Um, levels of leverage. You have to teach your team and show your team like how to get more leverage. You can't be the only one in the company that has leverage. The maximum form of leverage is showing your employees how to get leverage, you know."   [2:15:38]  "So, we call it the levels of leverage. Number one is doing it yourself. Then you document and train someone else. Then someone else is doing it. Then you have someone else documenting and training someone else. Like that's the ultimate form of leverage, right?"   [2:16:00]  "Like our sales management team is doing all the process documentation. Well, actually that's not true. They don't do process documentation. They give updates if they feel something's inaccurate. Our ops team does document process documentation, but they're doing all the training."   [2:16:20]  "So, you have to train them on how to know how to train and that's where you get the maximum leverage and output from the team."   [2:16:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Managing Your Manager"   NÚMEROS:  Múltiplos critérios de avaliação   VISUAL:  Checklist de como avaliar se seu gerente está fazendo bom trabalho   [2:16:38]  "And I was just going to say uh this last actual like educational note and we'll end it here. Um managing your manager. All right. So, uh first just a couple of notes that came to mind."   [2:16:55]  "First of all, you want a problem solver. You're not trying to go for problemless. You'll never get there. It's unattainable. really good problem solvers and standardize the way they solve problems."   [2:17:12]  "The second thing is remove lead quality from their vocabulary. Next thing is know specifically what each rep struggles with and know their current SIP. If they can't tell you the very specific thing that the rep is focused on for that week, they're not doing their job."   [2:17:35]  "They must have a pulse on pipeline and forecast. I want to know at all times like, hey, sales are a little bit slow right now. What do we have in the pipeline? If they can't tell me the forecasted report, maybe not by memory, but within a couple of minutes, you know, um, or tell me the last update, like, then there's something wrong. They should be intimately know that."   [2:18:00]  "Um, they should have the data and documentation to support every claim. I had a mentor a long time ago tell me this, and I like to say it often. He said, uh, I don't care what you think. I don't care how you feel. I only give a fuck what you do."   [2:18:20]  "And, uh, and that's that's what I that's what I say here. Like I don't really care what anybody thinks or feels. Like what actually is it? Like show me the evidence. Like you think that marketing's gone downhill. Like can you show me the evidence that you know to support that claim? So I'm a big fan of showing not telling."   [2:18:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Why I Do This - Referral Request"   NÚMEROS:  $7-8K+ ASP, $100K-200K+ monthly revenue requirement   VISUAL:  Account criteria e call-to-action para referências   [2:18:48]  "All right. So thank you for that. I'm going to close here with this and and then uh what I'll say well I'll ask even Jeremy like I want to do a Q&A for you guys but we will take a break. So maybe like we just take a break and for those who have questions I'll stay up here because that's the reason I'm here. But we'll take a break for sure."   [2:19:12]  "Um, but I wanted to say this. Why do I do this? Is it because I love Jeremy partly, but no, it's not. Um, it's cuz like anyone else, like I'm trying to build authority and awareness around my subject matter expertise to make money. Like I'm here because I want to make money like everybody else here."   [2:19:35]  "And so what I would ask you guys is just simply this. Like if I said anything today that your guys like, 'Wow, that was profound. That was prolific. This guy actually knows what he's talking about.' and you know somebody that needs done for you sales in the info space, just refer them. That's all I'm asking. That's what I would appreciate."   [2:20:00]  "That's the reason I try to pour I pour out all my secrets. I don't hold anything in. I'll ask answer any question that you have for that one purpose. Um our account criteria, like we can talk about that more in detail, but it has to be a good offer I believe in. It has to be integrity based. It has to have solid fulfillment."   [2:20:22]  "It needs to be at least like a seven or eight average sales price for the most part. They have to have market validation at least over 100 grand a month, preferably 200 grand a month. Um, but talk to me if it's at least 100. Predictable lead flow, credibility, they actually do the thing they teach, that type of stuff."   [2:20:45]  "Um, and yeah, you know, anyways, that's kind of the whole deal. My Instagram is troy.joshua. All right. Thank you."     📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO   NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS: $800K/mês em vendas de webinars (múltiplas marcas) $3.5M em challenge funnel $600K mês recorde (crescimento de $400K) $30K investimento em treinamento de vendas (comparação) $7 custo por call (Instagram shoutouts) $50 custo por call (exemplo de cálculo ROAS) $400 collected por call (exemplo) 8x ROAS individual por rep $150K em pipeline forecast (exemplo) 65-70% de KPIs estabilizados durante ramping 60-90 dias sales cycle (SaaS B2B) 14 dias de conteúdo de treinamento reduzido para 5 dias 3 horas de conteúdo para skills assessment 50% probabilidade como threshold de forecast 24-48 horas para follow-up calls 2 semanas sales cycle (exemplo) 3 syncs por semana 3 tipos de avaliação pré-phone 2 chances para passar assessments 4 dozen testimonials online 10% vs 20% close rate (comparação de performance) $100K-200K+ receita mensal mínima para clientes $7-8K+ average sales price mínimo   ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:   Core Four (4 Pilares Principais): Call Analysis - Tudo que você precisa saber está nas calls Pipeline Management - #1 lugar onde se perde dinheiro (revenue leakage) Performance Management - Custo mais alto = underperformers Training Center Optimization - Máxima alavancagem   Hiring & Onboarding: Processo de entrevista altamente competitivo e com alta fricção Talent acquisition tratada como customer acquisition (VSSLs, sales pages, testimonials) Skills assessment com conteúdo padronizado Sales directors fazem skills assessment para ownership desde dia 1 Onboarding imaculado tratado como customer fulfillment 5 dias até phones (reduzido de 14 dias)   Training: "The Big Three": Discovery, Pitch/Personalization, Closing Sequence Treinamento gamificado - unlock de níveis Talk track (não "script") 3 assessments antes de phones: Skills, Process, Product Knowledge Treinamento diário em nível de empresa   Call Analysis: Feedback quebrado em 3 fases baseado em performance tier Phase 1 (Non-validated/Ramping/Underperforming): Big Three + Technical Compliance Phase 2 (Stabilized): 10 Steps detalhados Phase 3 (Top Performers): Advanced feedback (Jedi mind tricks) AI-enabled call analysis com scorecards automáticos Lower thirds management - foco em underperformers, não top performers   Performance Management: Keystone Metrics: GDPC (Gross Dollars Per Call) e CDPC (Collected Dollars Per Call) Vanity metrics: Show rate, Close rate, ASP, Collection rate Conditional logic-based performance management (if X then Y) Ramping KPIs: 65-70% de stabilized KPIs Success Implementation Plans (SIPs) - não PIPs   Pipeline Management: Priority buckets no CRM (P1, P2, P3...) Remove critical thinking dos reps Management dashboards paralelos aos rep buckets Favorite reports: Hot List (7 days), Expected Close Past Due, Lost Deals Previously Forecasted, Qualified Loss Low Activity Forecasting baseado em 50%+ deal probability Weighted forecast Comments no CRM, não Slack/email   Validation Sequence (4 níveis): Lead Quality (com especificidade, não genérico) Rep Performance Process/Pitch/Talk Track Offer Design   Weekly Structure: WPM (Weekly Pulse Meeting) segundas-feiras 3 syncs por semana: Pipeline/Goals, Call Review/Game Tape, Product Knowledge/Brand Dynamics Management Tracker com múltiplas tabs: Call Review Log, Rep Development Log, Lead Insights, Objection Matrix, FAQ Matrix, Competitor Log, etc.   Levels of Leverage: Fazer você mesmo Documentar e treinar outro Outro está fazendo Outro documentando e treinando outro   ESTRUTURA:   Leadership Philosophy: Influence-based leadership vs Authority-based Sales principles = Leadership principles Vision exercises "Want the deal but don't need the deal" Fish rots from the head down   Management Tiers: Non-validated/Ramping Underperforming Stabilized Overperforming   Key Concepts: "Everything you need to know is on the calls" "Remove lead quality from vocabulary" (precisa especificidade) "Do better is not a plan" "Visibility is accountability's best friend" "Quantitative tells you what, Qualitative tells you why" "Simple scales, don't add complexity" "Skill vs Will" "Where energy goes, money flows" Emergency Close concept 24-48 hour follow-up rule (CRITICAL) Speed to lead applies to follow-ups too Don't turn warm into cold   Objection Handling Philosophy: Bad closers ignore objections Good closers handle objections Best closers anticipate and BLOCK objections before they come   Account Criteria: Info/education offers $7-8K+ ASP minimum $100K-200K+ monthly revenue Market validation Integrity-based Solid fulfillment Predictable lead flow Credibility Actually do what they teach   DADOS FINANCEIROS: Multiple $800K/month accounts $3.5M challenge funnel results $600K record month (from $400K) 8x ROAS examples on individual rep level Cost per call ranging from $7 (Instagram) to $50-100 (standard) $400 collected per call examples Performance delta calculation: lowest vs highest KPIs Most expensive thing: underperforming reps Spiffs for lost deals previously forecasted Commission structure tied to multi-metric performance   Technology Stack: Custom AI configuration for call analysis (months of development) Qualitative sales scorecard automated FTC compliance scorecard automated Technical compliance tracking Product knowledge scorecard Data quality dashboard with live feed ChatGPT-type technology for data queries CRM with smart views/filters/priority buckets Deal rotting features Forecasting reports Activity reports Offshore auditing team for data verification   DURAÇÃO:  2:21:00  FORMATO:  Apresentação presencial em mastermind (Miami)
